steps.title: Reconcile roadmap/backlog docs with current implementation and create status tracking
notes: Status tracker initialized.
Rewrote the roadmap and engineering backlog to reflect the evidence-first pipeline that already exists.
Captured explicit product-ready decisions for support-bundle behavior, redaction defaults, trust model, and Java 25 verification.

steps.title: Stabilize the canonical report contract and document it
notes: Added schemaVersion = 1 to the canonical AnalysisReport contract.
Documented the saved report bundle contract, top-level fields, compatibility rules, and privacy model in docs/report-contract.md.
Updated assembler, codec, and report-bundle tests to verify the stable JSON structure.

steps.title: Make Java 25 the explicit, fail-fast verification baseline
notes: Added Maven Enforcer so builds fail fast when Maven runs on Java older than 25.
Updated README guidance so contributors know Java 25 is required for compile and test verification.
Verified that the shell default Java 17 now fails clearly while Java 25 runs the full test suite successfully.

steps.title: Separate deterministic parser input from LLM-oriented truncation
notes: Artifact ingest now preserves full artifact content and classifies on the full file instead of a truncated excerpt.
Legacy prompt truncation moved to the fallback analyze, ask, and correlation prompt paths so structured findings retain full-fidelity evidence.
Verified with Java 25 compile and full test execution after adding loader regression coverage.

steps.title: Improve directory discovery, unsupported-file reporting, and artifact inventory
notes: Added deterministic support-bundle discovery inventory with explicit supported versus unsupported file tracking.
Directory analysis now preserves discovered artifact identity in the structured path and surfaces skipped files in console, text, Markdown, HTML, and JSON reports.
Verified the new inventory behavior with Java 25 compile and full test execution, including loader, report-bundle, and DiagnosticData metadata round-trip coverage.

steps.title: Complete remaining parser, rule, and comparison coverage for v1 artifacts
notes: Inspected the already-dirty parser and rule files and chose NMT as the safest high-value next slice because the parser already exposed more structured signals than the rules were using.
Extended NMT parser evidence to include category, class-summary, thread-summary, and metaspace-summary anchors for both summary and diff snapshots.
Expanded NMT rule coverage with direct-evidence metaspace findings, class-metadata growth detection, code-cache pressure detection, and thread-pressure follow-up actions.

Completed and verified the hs_err slice: crash classification now distinguishes native allocation failures, captures VM OOM header details and current-thread context, and emits agent-driven native OOM and compiler-thread findings.

Completed and verified the GC slice: parser coverage now includes pause distribution, ZGC cycle summaries, allocation stalls, MMU minima, peak post-GC occupancy, and metaspace snapshots, and the assessor now emits allocation-stall and metaspace-triggered full-GC findings with explicit evidence.

Completed and verified the heap histogram slice: parser coverage now emits payload, cache-like, and collection scaffolding summaries; single-artifact rules now flag likely retention patterns with guided actions; and the structured comparator now interprets growth as cache and collection retention instead of only a generic class delta.

Completed and verified the pmap slice: parser coverage now captures virtual-versus-resident summaries, per-category RSS and dirty breakdowns, largest resident mappings, and reserved-gap evidence; single-artifact rules now distinguish anonymous pressure from virtual-versus-resident mismatch; and the structured comparator now uses RSS growth when available while separating reserved-only expansion from resident footprint growth.
Workstream 6 is now complete. Next focus: workstream 7 correlation hardening.

steps.title: Strengthen structured comparison and cross-artifact correlation
notes: Confirmed that typed comparison is already the active structured path for the supported comparison artifact types and kept that path intact.

Expanded multi-artifact correlation for metaspace-plus-class pressure, native OOM confirmation, mixed heap-plus-native pressure, and broader crash-under-distress coverage using the richer deterministic rule findings from GC, NMT, heap histogram, pmap, and hs_err artifacts.
Fixed a correlation chaining bug where follow-on correlations could never see previously emitted correlation findings because the correlator only checked the original rule findings.
Correlation findings now derive evidence IDs and contributing artifact paths from the findings that actually triggered them instead of relying on a small hardcoded evidence list.

When no unified correlation can be emitted, the correlation summary now calls out missing companion evidence for metaspace pressure, native OOM confirmation, and mixed heap-versus-native interpretation.

steps.title: Make the structured path the default runtime experience
notes: Structured routing became the default runtime path for analyze, compare, correlate, and ask on supported v1 artifacts as the first step toward retiring the older prompt-first branch.

The CLI now preserves or resolves structured report context during load and ask flows, and it auto-generates a structured report when a supported loaded artifact is questioned without a matching saved report.
That runtime demotion laid the groundwork for the later step that removed the remaining prompt-first compatibility path entirely.

steps.title: Add report redaction, richer renderers, and product-ready bundle behavior
notes: Shareable report formats now apply the internal-safe-v1 redaction profile by default, covering hostnames and IPs, filesystem paths, explicit command-line fields, and obvious sensitive environment-style assignments while leaving report.json full fidelity.

Console, Markdown, and HTML renderers now surface richer incident context with report metadata, correlation summaries, per-finding evidence details, action rationale and steps, missing-data guidance, and shareability notices.
The canonical JSON report now includes derived reportMetadata so future tooling can see bundle counts and the active shareable redaction profile without scraping human-readable renderings.
Verified with focused report-bundle and pipeline tests plus full Java 25 compile and test execution.

steps.title: Expand trust gates, regression coverage, and report stability verification
notes: Added explicit regression coverage for unknown file classification, corrupt saved report bundles, shareable redaction behavior, and an exact canonical JSON snapshot.

The new snapshot test caught a real determinism bug: artifactSummaries ordering was unstable because AnalysisReport used Map.copyOf for summary maps. That path now preserves LinkedHashMap order in the canonical JSON contract.
No-provider structured behavior is now covered across the pipeline and structured Q and A tests through existing null-chat-model scenarios plus the new trust-gate suite.
Refreshed the roadmap and engineering backlog headers so the repo docs now state that the v1 product-ready CLI workstreams are complete and point future work toward post-v1 expansion.

steps.title: Automate Java 25 verification for contributors and CI
notes: Added a shared scripts/verify-java25.sh entrypoint so local verification and CI now execute the same Java 25 compile and full-test path.
Added a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the shared Java 25 verification script on pushes, pull requests, and manual dispatches using setup-java with Maven dependency caching.
Documented the workflow in README.md and CONTRIBUTING.md, and refreshed the roadmap and backlog so CI automation is no longer described as future work.

Updated imports, package declarations, data-layer adapters, tests, and documentation while keeping modelproviders as the dedicated AI-model integration package.

steps.title: Promote specialist agents to the primary runtime path
notes: Added an agent-driven orchestration layer that builds bounded artifact, comparison, and correlation starting context from deterministic parsing, assessor output, evidence anchors, and curated excerpts before invoking specialist agents.

The CLI now routes supported analyze, compare, correlate, and auto-generated ask context through specialist-agent orchestration instead of treating AI as a narrative add-on to deterministic pipelines.
Expanded specialist coverage with JFR, thread-dump, container-memory, and OOM or restart-signal agents so the current supported artifact surface now has an explicit agent path.

Updated report rendering and saved-report follow-up so specialist-agent analysis appears as a first-class output, then verified end to end with JAVA_HOME=/Users/poonam/jdks/jdk-25.jdk/Contents/Home PATH="$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH" bash scripts/verify-java25.sh, which passed with 138 tests.

steps.title: Capture agent traceability and add AI-agent trust gates
notes: Renamed the runtime vocabulary from Grounded to AgentDriven across orchestration, prompts, renderers, CLI help text, tests, and roadmap or status docs so the agent-first terminology matches the product direction.

Extended the canonical AnalysisReport contract and report.json projection with agentTraceability plus lightweight quality-gate results, while keeping schemaVersion 1 and backward-compatible loading for older saved bundles.

The agent-driven orchestrator now records whether the selected narrative came from a specialist agent, the correlation synthesis agent, the bounded report summarizer, or a deterministic fallback, along with artifact paths, evidence IDs, and initial trust checks.

Console, Markdown, HTML, and ask flows now surface the saved traceability trail, and focused regression tests cover specialist success, fallback summarizer selection after a blank agent response, JSON snapshot stability, and renderer exposure.

steps.title: Deepen supervisor traceability and add reference incident evaluation bundles
notes: Extended the canonical AnalysisReport contract, JSON codec, catalog metadata, and human-readable renderers with saved supervisorTrace data while keeping schemaVersion 1 and backward-compatible loading for older bundles.

The agent-driven orchestrator now persists explainable workflow steps for single-artifact, compare, and correlate flows, including artifact grounding, deterministic comparison or correlation evaluation, specialist observation selection, and final synthesis selection.
ask responses and shareable report renderings now surface the saved supervisor workflow so users can inspect how the multi-agent runtime chose specialists and incident synthesis output.

Added checked-in reference incident manifests plus an end-to-end regression harness that validates compare and correlate workflows, selected agents, required findings, supervisor step coverage, and evidence discipline rather than only parser determinism.

Refreshed current report-bundle fixtures and verification coverage, then verified end to end with JAVA_HOME=/Users/poonam/jdks/jdk-25.jdk/Contents/Home PATH="$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH" bash scripts/verify-java25.sh, which passed with 142 tests.

steps.title: Capture provider/model traceability and broaden release-blocking trust thresholds
notes: Added an explicit saved-report agentParticipationSummary projection plus renderer-visible Analysis Path summaries so users and downstream tooling can tell whether an AI agent was attempted, whether it was selected for the final narrative, and whether the final narrative was LLM-backed.

Added a dedicated user-facing console renderer for live CLI use so analyze, compare, correlate, and default text report viewing now show full-fidelity troubleshooting guidance instead of the safe-share redacted report format and internal orchestration detail.
Updated the specialist-agent and fallback summarizer prompts to favor user-friendly troubleshooting structure with summary, key metrics, likely issues, recommended actions, and next steps.

Tightened the user-facing contract so analyze, compare, correlate, report loading or showing, and ask now refuse fallback-only or deterministic-only reports; deterministic parsing remains internal grounding and no troubleshooting analysis is shown or saved unless a specialist or synthesis AI agent is selected for the final narrative.
The live user renderer and structured question-answering path now reject fallback-only reports, and ask no longer falls back to deterministic answers when no live AI model is available.
Changed the CLI startup default provider from OCI to Ollama so local model usage is the first runtime path, while preserving OCI as the fallback provider if Ollama is unavailable at launch.

Realigned the agent packets so specialist and synthesis agents now receive structured extracted context, evidence anchors, and chunked source context rather than deterministic findings or recommended actions, and correlation no longer injects deterministic fallback observations into the synthesis packet.

The live user renderer now prefers the AI narrative's own Summary, Key metrics, Likely issues, Recommended actions, and Next steps sections instead of reprinting deterministic findings and actions as the primary user guidance.

Updated specialist-agent and fallback summarizer prompts so user-facing narratives refer directly to GC logs, JFR recordings, thread dumps, crash logs, memory snapshots, and combined diagnostics instead of internal packet-style vocabulary.

Added a blocking user-language quality gate so narratives that expose internal orchestration terms such as "the packet suggests" are rejected and only another AI-written narrative path can be selected for the user view.
Verified the prompt cleanup and user-language guard with JAVA_HOME=/Users/poonam/jdks/jdk-25.jdk/Contents/Home PATH="$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH" bash scripts/verify-java25.sh, which passed with 150 tests.

Removed remaining runtime uses of the phrase agent-driven for internal AI context packets and user-facing report/help text, replacing them with diagnostic context, AI analysis, or saved analysis report wording so the extraction layer reads as support for agent analysis rather than a product concept.

Extended saved agent traceability and selected supervisor trace steps with explicit provider, model, model-family, and template-version execution metadata, and projected the selected runtime identity into report metadata and catalog summaries.
Added a blocking model-execution-traceability gate so an AI narrative cannot become the accepted user-facing output unless provider, model, and template metadata were captured alongside it.

Expanded reference incident release coverage to include a single-artifact GC workflow and hardened the regression harness so the selected AI narrative and selected supervisor trace step must both preserve model-execution traceability.

Verified the step-19 completion with JAVA_HOME=/Users/poonam/jdks/jdk-25.jdk/Contents/Home PATH="$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH" bash scripts/verify-java25.sh, which passed with 161 tests after fixing shareable redaction false positives for model and template identifiers.

steps.title: Implement hybrid diagnostic context, curated agent retrieval, and focused computation tooling
notes: Added a shared internal diagnostic-context layer that turns fully parsed artifacts into bounded starting context for agents, including structured facts, diagnostic highlights, representative raw or derived slices, and explicit coverage metadata.
Introduced a shared indexer, curated retriever, and focused computation service so agents can expand the same artifact safely instead of relying on arbitrary file reads or deterministic findings.

Refactored the diagnostic-context builder and orchestrator so specialist and correlation agents now receive bounded starting context plus session-aware retrieval or computation tools with enforced per-workflow budgets.
Extended internal traceability and supervisor trace data with tool invocation logging while keeping user-facing output clean and troubleshooting-focused.
Added a coverage-aware confidence gate so highly certain narratives from truncated starting context are rejected unless the agent retrieved more context or explicitly acknowledged uncertainty.

Expanded agent tool coverage across GC, JFR, thread dump, hs_err, NMT, heap histogram, pmap, container-memory, OOM signal, and correlation workflows, and updated prompts so agents treat the starting context as bounded and tool-expandable.
Added regression coverage for bounded context construction, JFR derived-only safety, tool runtime logging and budget enforcement, and the new coverage-aware quality gate.
Verified end to end with JAVA_HOME=/Users/poonam/jdks/jdk-25.jdk/Contents/Home PATH="$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH" bash scripts/verify-java25.sh, which passed with 156 tests.

steps.title: Remove obsolete pre-agent runtime code and fallback summarizer wiring
notes: Removed the unused pre-agent pipeline package and its tests because the CLI now routes only through the specialist-agent orchestrator.

Deleted the old StructuredReportSummarizer class and removed fallback-summarizer selection from analyze, compare, and correlate so the live runtime no longer produces non-agent troubleshooting narratives.
Kept legacy fallback narrative-source enum values and renderer coverage only for saved-report compatibility, while verifying the cleaned-up runtime with the full Java 25 verification path.

steps.title: Harden bounded diagnostic context completeness and omitted-context expansion
notes: Raised the first-pass context caps and preserved complete raw text coverage internally by indexing full text artifacts into ordered raw chunks rather than only a few representative windows.

Added exact omitted-slice retrieval by sliceId, made blank retrieval prefer omitted context, and tightened moreAvailable signaling so agents get clearer completeness feedback when expanding bounded context.

Strengthened the coverage-aware confidence gate so bounded contexts now require retrieval before a narrative can be accepted, and highly certain narratives are rejected when retrieved context still reports omissions, truncation, or parse gaps.

Expanded per-workflow tool budgets, compacted coverage rendering in the starting context, and updated specialist prompts and retrieval tool descriptions so agents are told how to fetch omitted slices with blank selectors or sliceId queries.

Added regression coverage for raw-chunk preservation, omitted-slice retrieval, stricter coverage gating, and updated reference-incident expectations for retrieval-dependent bundles under the non-tooling routing stub.
Verified end to end with JAVA_HOME=/Users/poonam/jdks/jdk-25.jdk/Contents/Home PATH="$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH" bash scripts/verify-java25.sh, which passed with 144 tests.

steps.title: Complete the second-pass context hardening for lossless retrieval and artifact-aware gating
notes: Preserved full internal structured, raw, and derived slice content for retrieval and moved truncation to the bounded starting-context copies so agents can still reopen the complete material later.

Added session-scoped seen-slice tracking so blank retrieval actually progresses across omitted context instead of repeating the first omitted block or slice, and wired generic JFR retrieval through the richer JFR selector contract.

Added slice paging with offset/chars, explicit no-match behavior for specific selectors, and updated packet hints plus agent tool or prompt guidance so long slices can be reopened directly without exposing internal workflow language.

Refined the coverage-aware confidence gate to reason per artifact using the latest retrieval state rather than any historical incomplete expansion, while distinguishing retrievable omissions from parser gaps that require explicit uncertainty.

Updated regression coverage and the compare reference-incident expectation for the stricter retrieval-aware acceptance policy, then verified end to end with JAVA_HOME=/Users/poonam/jdks/jdk-25.jdk/Contents/Home PATH="$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH" bash scripts/verify-java25.sh, which passed with 152 tests.

steps.title: Expand first-pass agent context and preserve fuller JFR-derived detail
notes: Made starting-context budgets model-aware so configured Ollama or OCI runs can front-load more structured slices, representative slices, and highlight detail without changing the no-arg deterministic test baseline.

Expanded the internal JFR representation from narrow top-N summaries to fuller observed-event catalogs, declared event metadata, and untruncated ranked hotspot, allocation, and old-object lists so agents can page through much richer binary-derived context.

Rebalanced text-artifact representative slices toward broader chronology, added retrievable structured highlight blocks, raised retrieval and computation page sizes plus tool budgets, removed hidden truncation in rendered metadata and highlight sections, and trimmed duplicate attribute rendering so more of the first-pass prompt budget goes to unique diagnostic content.
Verified end to end with JAVA_HOME=/Users/poonam/jdks/jdk-25.jdk/Contents/Home PATH="$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH" bash scripts/verify-java25.sh, which passed with 154 tests.

notes: Added model-aware starting-context budget selection that now distinguishes compact local Ollama models from stronger models instead of relying only on approximate context-window size.

Compact local models such as llama3.2 now receive a tighter first-pass mix of highlights, structured slices, representative slices, and per-slice character caps, while larger local models and stronger OCI-backed models keep the richer budget path.
Wired the orchestrator through the new model-aware budget selector and added regression coverage for both budget selection and prompt-size differences between llama3.2 and stronger models.

steps.title: Polish packaging, startup workflow, and user guidance
notes: Promoted one-shot commands to the primary CLI UX, with `jtroubleshoot shell` as the explicit stateful mode instead of dropping every user into an interactive loop.

Reworked the CLI around clearer task verbs: one-shot `analyze`, `compare`, `correlate`, `ask`, and `reports` commands, plus shell-oriented `open`, `open-report`, `show`, `context`, and `clear` flows with a context-aware prompt.

Added a real repo launcher at `bin/jtroubleshoot`, including packaged-jar discovery and a Java 25 guardrail that produces a clean guidance message instead of a class-version stack trace when the shell default Java is too old.

notes: Added top-level `--provider <oci|ollama>` and `--model <name>` flags that apply to one-shot commands and to shell startup, without introducing persistent config yet.

Removed eager provider initialization from CLI startup and replaced it with lazy selected-model creation, so `help`, `status`, `version`, and `shell` startup can resolve provider or model selection without forcing AI model initialization.

Updated provider factories to accept per-command model overrides, refreshed CLI help and README examples, and extended the architecture docs so the control flow now reflects global option parsing plus lazy model activation.

steps.title: Explain rejected AI narratives clearly and harden the thread-dump deadlock path
notes: Replaced the generic CLI no-analysis message with a user-facing rejection summary that explains whether the AI response was empty, under-contextualized, or blocked for another acceptance reason, and it now shows the active provider/model plus a concrete next step.

Carried real AI invocation failure details through the quality gate path so provider/model call failures now surface in the CLI rejection message instead of collapsing into a generic empty-response message.
Hardened the thread-dump path so small deadlock dumps that fit comfortably in first-pass context are front-loaded completely for the agent instead of being rejected for missing additional retrieval.
